response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with a genuine developer-tooling background — including work on the LabWindows/CVI IDE and authoring test runners and assertion libraries — but the role's core requirements are not met. There is no evidence of Kotlin, Spring, or deep JVM expertise; Java appears only briefly and long ago, with the bulk of his recent work in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. For a senior IC role centered on enterprise Java/Kotlin tooling, the ecosystem gap is too large despite the strong general engineering craft.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI) and clear interest in language tooling
Track record building developer tools: test runners, assertion libraries, MCP code generators, CI/dev-ops infrastructure
Strong engineering craft: TDD, low-latency systems at scale, open-source maintainership over many years
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin or Spring ecosystem experience, both required qualifications
Java exposure is minor and dated (side projects at Altom ~2016-2018), with no deep JVM work evident
Recent career centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang web platforms, far from enterprise JVM tooling

key_factors.factor: Recent stack misaligned with JVM ecosystem
